tossups.question: A product of this name that was designed "to become an omnimedium" was publicly demonstrated with both Amelia Earhart and Eleanor Roosevelt before the company went bankrupt. A scheme to take half-hour naps once every six hours was dubbed this kind of "sleep." This is the first word in the name of a map profiled in Life magazine that projected onto an icosahedron to avoid some of the distortions in a Mercator projection. Alec Nevala-Lee documented the numerous fatal accidents of a car of this name that had only a single rear wheel. An inventor had to throw pebbles at kids to keep them from climbing onto the roof of a structure of this name in Carbondale, Illinois. That house of this name had (*) hexagonal walls making it hard to hang pictures. For 10 points, name this word used in a number of products dreamed up by Buckminster Fuller, which combines "dynamic" with two other words.
tossups.answer: Dymaxion [accept Dymaxion car or Dymaxion house or Dymaxion map or Dymaxion sleep]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Dymaxion [accept Dymaxion car or Dymaxion house or Dymaxion map or Dymaxion sleep]
tossups.question_sanitized: A product of this name that was designed "to become an omnimedium" was publicly demonstrated with both Amelia Earhart and Eleanor Roosevelt before the company went bankrupt. A scheme to take half-hour naps once every six hours was dubbed this kind of "sleep." This is the first word in the name of a map profiled in Life magazine that projected onto an icosahedron to avoid some of the distortions in a Mercator projection. Alec Nevala-Lee documented the numerous fatal accidents of a car of this name that had only a single rear wheel. An inventor had to throw pebbles at kids to keep them from climbing onto the roof of a structure of this name in Carbondale, Illinois. That house of this name had (*) hexagonal walls making it hard to hang pictures. For 10 points, name this word used in a number of products dreamed up by Buckminster Fuller, which combines "dynamic" with two other words.

bonuses.leadin: This term names a three-wheeled car that its designer intended to be a “zoomobile” able to “hop off the road at will” and fly into a new place in traffic. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this portmanteau term that names a “deployment unit” inspired by steel grain bins used in Midwest agriculture. Its coiner also designed a Wichita house that used the “dome effect” to regulate its temperature and a “fogger” in place of a shower.
This thinker recorded his activities every 15 minutes for 63 years in his scrapbook Dymaxion Chronofile. He developed synergetics and designed the Montreal Biosphère.

Fuller also used “Dymaxion” to name one of these things that used an unfolded icosahedron to remove the concept of direction. He claimed that it distorts the global image less than their Mercator and Robinson types.
answers: dymaxion [accept Dymaxion Car or Dymaxion Deployment Unit or Dymaxion House] (Dymaxion is a portmanteau of dynamic, maximum, and tension.)

This thinker recorded his activities every 15 minutes for 63 years in his scrapbook Dymaxion Chronofile. He developed synergetics and designed the Montreal Biosphere.
